{'text': '兵庫県姫路市網干区宮内193', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '兵庫県たつの市揖保町中臣', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:魚吹八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11673954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Usuki Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[File:Rōmon of Usuki Hachiman Shrine.jpg|260px]]
Romon gate |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}{{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}Miyauchi 193 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = Tsu-no-miya |札所等 = |例祭 = October 21-22 |神事 = Bushin Festival (last Saturday of March) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Usuki
Hachiman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Usuki Hachiman Shrine''' (うすきはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at Miyauchi 193, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also called "Tsu-no-miya." It is said to have been founded by divine oracle during {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. It is one of the candidate identifications for {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine|Q17226544|WD=Q17226544|1=Nakatomi Itate Shrine}}, a {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. A {{ill|lt=chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} said to have been lifted by {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Unosuke|Q11354047|WD=Q11354047|1=Sannomiya Unosuke}} (an {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} strongman/acrobat) remains, and a statue of Unosuke is also erected. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} === Festivals === * Bushin Festival - last Saturday of {{ill|lt=March|Q110|WD=Q110|1=March}} (Himeji City designated intangible folk cultural property) * Shokon Festival - April 22 * Sento Festival - {{ill|lt=July 14|Q2691|WD=Q2691|1=July 14}} * Autumn annual festival - {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}-{{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}} === Lantern Festival === The autumn annual festival is called the '''Lantern Festival'''. Eighteen yatai, four danjiri, and one lion-danjiri participate; it has the largest number of parishioners in the Banshu region{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}}, the {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Harima, has 5 yatai; {{ill|lt=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine|Q11529360|WD=Q11529360|1=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine}} known for the {{ill|lt=Nada no Kenka Matsuri|Q11566794|WD=Q11566794|1=Nada no kenka matsuri}} has 7; most other local shrines have single-digit counts, while Usuki has 23 total (yatai/danjiri/lion-danjiri).. It is one of the representative festivals of {{ill|lt=Banshu autumn festivals|Q11498705|WD=Q11498705|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri}} and is designated an intangible folk cultural property of Hyogo Prefecture (2007). The festival is said to originate from travel lanterns offered as sacred lights for three mikoshi carrying the three principal deities during their procession to the otabisho. After the war, it gradually became more intense, eventually featuring vigorous "neri" performances before shrine entry[https://www.himeji-kanko.jp/event/13 Lantern Festival ] Himeji Tourism Navi "Himenomichi". ;Yoimiya (October 21) The famous '''chochin neri''' crushes lanterns hung from tips of green bamboo poles about 3 meters long. From evening onward, this is performed by seven coastal districts in front of the romon and on the precinct approach. Other districts also hold yatai neri from daytime, with joint performances at sites including in front of JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|1=Aboshi Station}} and {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Elementary School|Q11447488|WD=Q11447488|1=Katsuhara Elementary School}}. Danjiri districts begin pulling danjiri toward the otabisho around noon. Around 22:00, three mikoshi and a gold gohei leave the main hall for the west otabisho ("Nishi no Baba") (togyo), where rites are performed (shinko-sai). ;Hirumiya (October 22) From early morning, yatai and others from each district gather at the otabisho around 8-9 a.m. for joint neri. In the afternoon, mikoshi and gold gohei return to the shrine (kango), and yatai move to the shrine in prearranged order. Highlights include yatai neri lifting floats high with the shout "Chosa," lion dance, and danjiri performances. At the 2019 hirumiya, before kango, a parade celebrating the emperor's accession was held in connection with that day's {{ill|lt=Sokuirei Seiden no Gi|Q20041008|WD=Q20041008|1=Enthronement ceremony of the Japanese Emperor}}. ;Festival areas The following 24 towns: * Himeji {{ill|lt=Otsu Ward|Q11437241|WD=Q11437241|1=Ōtsu}} - '''Yoshimi''', '''Hiramatsu''', Temma, Nagamatsu, Nishi-doi * Himeji {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Ward|Q11400189|WD=Q11400189|1=Katsuhara}} - Asahi-dani, Kumami, Miyata, Yoro * Himeji Aboshi Ward - '''Oeshima''', '''Kakichi''', '''Okinohama''', '''Shinzaike''', '''Yokohama''', Sakaide, Sakaue, Takada, Tsuichiba-kita, Tsuichiba, Fukui, Waku, Tai, Miyauchi * {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Hyogo)|Q1155186|WD=Q1155186|lt=Taishi Town|1=Taishi, Hyōgo}} - Itoi **Okinohama, Shinzaike, Yokohama, and Kakichi are danjiri districts; Asahi-dani is lion-danjiri. Miyauchi leads togyo/kango, and Waku with Ekimae conducts joint yatai procession. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:兵庫県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:兵庫県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:網干]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is one of the candidate identifications for {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine|Q17226544|WD=Q17226544|1=Nakatomi Itate Shrine}}, a {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. A {{ill|lt=chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} said to have been lifted by {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Unosuke|Q11354047|WD=Q11354047|1=Sannomiya Unosuke}} (an {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} strongman/acrobat) remains, and a statue of Unosuke is also erected. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} === Festivals === * Bushin Festival - last Saturday of {{ill|lt=March|Q110|WD=Q110|1=March}} (Himeji City designated intangible folk cultural property) * Shokon Festival - April 22 * Sento Festival - {{ill|lt=July 14|Q2691|WD=Q2691|1=July 14}} * Autumn annual festival - {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}-{{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}} === Lantern Festival === The autumn annual festival is called the '''Lantern Festival'''. Eighteen yatai, four danjiri, and one lion-danjiri participate; it has the largest number of parishioners in the Banshu region{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}}, the {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Harima, has 5 yatai; {{ill|lt=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine|Q11529360|WD=Q11529360|1=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine}} known for the {{ill|lt=Nada no Kenka Matsuri|Q11566794|WD=Q11566794|1=Nada no kenka matsuri}} has 7; most other local shrines have single-digit counts, while Usuki has 23 total (yatai/danjiri/lion-danjiri).. It is one of the representative festivals of {{ill|lt=Banshu autumn festivals|Q11498705|WD=Q11498705|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri}} and is designated an intangible folk cultural property of Hyogo Prefecture (2007). The festival is said to originate from travel lanterns offered as sacred lights for three mikoshi carrying the three principal deities during their procession to the otabisho. After the war, it gradually became more intense, eventually featuring vigorous "neri" performances before shrine entry[https://www.himeji-kanko.jp/event/13 Lantern Festival ] Himeji Tourism Navi "Himenomichi". ;Yoimiya (October 21) The famous '''chochin neri''' crushes lanterns hung from tips of green bamboo poles about 3 meters long. From evening onward, this is performed by seven coastal districts in front of the romon and on the precinct approach. Other districts also hold yatai neri from daytime, with joint performances at sites including in front of JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|1=Aboshi Station}} and {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Elementary School|Q11447488|WD=Q11447488|1=Katsuhara Elementary School}}. Danjiri districts begin pulling danjiri toward the otabisho around noon. Around 22:00, three mikoshi and a gold gohei leave the main hall for the west otabisho ("Nishi no Baba") (togyo), where rites are performed (shinko-sai). ;Hirumiya (October 22) From early morning, yatai and others from each district gather at the otabisho around 8-9 a.m. for joint neri. In the afternoon, mikoshi and gold gohei return to the shrine (kango), and yatai move to the shrine in prearranged order. Highlights include yatai neri lifting floats high with the shout "Chosa," lion dance, and danjiri performances. At the 2019 hirumiya, before kango, a parade celebrating the emperor's accession was held in connection with that day's {{ill|lt=Sokuirei Seiden no Gi|Q20041008|WD=Q20041008|1=Enthronement ceremony of the Japanese Emperor}}. ;Festival areas The following 24 towns: * Himeji {{ill|lt=Otsu Ward|Q11437241|WD=Q11437241|1=Ōtsu}} - '''Yoshimi''', '''Hiramatsu''', Temma, Nagamatsu, Nishi-doi * Himeji {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Ward|Q11400189|WD=Q11400189|1=Katsuhara}} - Asahi-dani, Kumami, Miyata, Yoro * Himeji Aboshi Ward - '''Oeshima''', '''Kakichi''', '''Okinohama''', '''Shinzaike''', '''Yokohama''', Sakaide, Sakaue, Takada, Tsuichiba-kita, Tsuichiba, Fukui, Waku, Tai, Miyauchi * {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Hyogo)|Q1155186|WD=Q1155186|lt=Taishi Town|1=Taishi, Hyōgo}} - Itoi **Okinohama, Shinzaike, Yokohama, and Kakichi are danjiri districts; Asahi-dani is lion-danjiri. Miyauchi leads togyo/kango, and Waku with Ekimae conducts joint yatai procession. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:兵庫県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:網干]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is said to have been founded by divine oracle during {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:網干]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{'text': '兵庫県姫路市網干区宮内193', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '兵庫県たつの市揖保町中臣', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:魚吹八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11673954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Usuki Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[File:Rōmon of Usuki Hachiman Shrine.jpg|260px]]
Romon gate |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}{{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}Miyauchi 193 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = Tsu-no-miya |札所等 = |例祭 = October 21-22 |神事 = Bushin Festival (last Saturday of March) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Usuki
Hachiman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Usuki Hachiman Shrine''' (うすきはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at Miyauchi 193, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also called "Tsu-no-miya." It is said to have been founded by divine oracle during {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. It is one of the candidate identifications for {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine|Q17226544|WD=Q17226544|1=Nakatomi Itate Shrine}}, a {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. A {{ill|lt=chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} said to have been lifted by {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Unosuke|Q11354047|WD=Q11354047|1=Sannomiya Unosuke}} (an {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} strongman/acrobat) remains, and a statue of Unosuke is also erected. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} === Festivals === * Bushin Festival - last Saturday of {{ill|lt=March|Q110|WD=Q110|1=March}} (Himeji City designated intangible folk cultural property) * Shokon Festival - April 22 * Sento Festival - {{ill|lt=July 14|Q2691|WD=Q2691|1=July 14}} * Autumn annual festival - {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}-{{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}} === Lantern Festival === The autumn annual festival is called the '''Lantern Festival'''. Eighteen yatai, four danjiri, and one lion-danjiri participate; it has the largest number of parishioners in the Banshu region{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}}, the {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Harima, has 5 yatai; {{ill|lt=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine|Q11529360|WD=Q11529360|1=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine}} known for the {{ill|lt=Nada no Kenka Matsuri|Q11566794|WD=Q11566794|1=Nada no kenka matsuri}} has 7; most other local shrines have single-digit counts, while Usuki has 23 total (yatai/danjiri/lion-danjiri).. It is one of the representative festivals of {{ill|lt=Banshu autumn festivals|Q11498705|WD=Q11498705|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri}} and is designated an intangible folk cultural property of Hyogo Prefecture (2007). The festival is said to originate from travel lanterns offered as sacred lights for three mikoshi carrying the three principal deities during their procession to the otabisho. After the war, it gradually became more intense, eventually featuring vigorous "neri" performances before shrine entry[https://www.himeji-kanko.jp/event/13 Lantern Festival ] Himeji Tourism Navi "Himenomichi". ;Yoimiya (October 21) The famous '''chochin neri''' crushes lanterns hung from tips of green bamboo poles about 3 meters long. From evening onward, this is performed by seven coastal districts in front of the romon and on the precinct approach. Other districts also hold yatai neri from daytime, with joint performances at sites including in front of JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|1=Aboshi Station}} and {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Elementary School|Q11447488|WD=Q11447488|1=Katsuhara Elementary School}}. Danjiri districts begin pulling danjiri toward the otabisho around noon. Around 22:00, three mikoshi and a gold gohei leave the main hall for the west otabisho ("Nishi no Baba") (togyo), where rites are performed (shinko-sai). ;Hirumiya (October 22) From early morning, yatai and others from each district gather at the otabisho around 8-9 a.m. for joint neri. In the afternoon, mikoshi and gold gohei return to the shrine (kango), and yatai move to the shrine in prearranged order. Highlights include yatai neri lifting floats high with the shout "Chosa," lion dance, and danjiri performances. At the 2019 hirumiya, before kango, a parade celebrating the emperor's accession was held in connection with that day's {{ill|lt=Sokuirei Seiden no Gi|Q20041008|WD=Q20041008|1=Enthronement ceremony of the Japanese Emperor}}. ;Festival areas The following 24 towns: * Himeji {{ill|lt=Otsu Ward|Q11437241|WD=Q11437241|1=Ōtsu}} - '''Yoshimi''', '''Hiramatsu''', Temma, Nagamatsu, Nishi-doi * Himeji {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Ward|Q11400189|WD=Q11400189|1=Katsuhara}} - Asahi-dani, Kumami, Miyata, Yoro * Himeji Aboshi Ward - '''Oeshima''', '''Kakichi''', '''Okinohama''', '''Shinzaike''', '''Yokohama''', Sakaide, Sakaue, Takada, Tsuichiba-kita, Tsuichiba, Fukui, Waku, Tai, Miyauchi * {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Hyogo)|Q1155186|WD=Q1155186|lt=Taishi Town|1=Taishi, Hyōgo}} - Itoi **Okinohama, Shinzaike, Yokohama, and Kakichi are danjiri districts; Asahi-dani is lion-danjiri. Miyauchi leads togyo/kango, and Waku with Ekimae conducts joint yatai procession. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:網干]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{'text': '兵庫県姫路市網干区宮内193', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '兵庫県たつの市揖保町中臣', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:魚吹八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11673954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Usuki Hachiman Shrine |画像 = [[File:Rōmon of Usuki Hachiman Shrine.jpg|260px]]
Romon gate |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}{{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}Miyauchi 193 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = Tsu-no-miya |札所等 = |例祭 = October 21-22 |神事 = Bushin Festival (last Saturday of March) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Usuki
Hachiman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Usuki Hachiman Shrine''' (うすきはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at Miyauchi 193, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also called "Tsu-no-miya." It is said to have been founded by divine oracle during {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. It is one of the candidate identifications for {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine|Q17226544|WD=Q17226544|1=Nakatomi Itate Shrine}}, a {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. A {{ill|lt=chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} said to have been lifted by {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Unosuke|Q11354047|WD=Q11354047|1=Sannomiya Unosuke}} (an {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} strongman/acrobat) remains, and a statue of Unosuke is also erected. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} === Festivals === * Bushin Festival - last Saturday of {{ill|lt=March|Q110|WD=Q110|1=March}} (Himeji City designated intangible folk cultural property) * Shokon Festival - April 22 * Sento Festival - {{ill|lt=July 14|Q2691|WD=Q2691|1=July 14}} * Autumn annual festival - {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}-{{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}} === Lantern Festival === The autumn annual festival is called the '''Lantern Festival'''. Eighteen yatai, four danjiri, and one lion-danjiri participate; it has the largest number of parishioners in the Banshu region{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}}, the {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Harima, has 5 yatai; {{ill|lt=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine|Q11529360|WD=Q11529360|1=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine}} known for the {{ill|lt=Nada no Kenka Matsuri|Q11566794|WD=Q11566794|1=Nada no kenka matsuri}} has 7; most other local shrines have single-digit counts, while Usuki has 23 total (yatai/danjiri/lion-danjiri).. It is one of the representative festivals of {{ill|lt=Banshu autumn festivals|Q11498705|WD=Q11498705|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri}} and is designated an intangible folk cultural property of Hyogo Prefecture (2007). The festival is said to originate from travel lanterns offered as sacred lights for three mikoshi carrying the three principal deities during their procession to the otabisho. After the war, it gradually became more intense, eventually featuring vigorous "neri" performances before shrine entry[https://www.himeji-kanko.jp/event/13 Lantern Festival ] Himeji Tourism Navi "Himenomichi". ;Yoimiya (October 21) The famous '''chochin neri''' crushes lanterns hung from tips of green bamboo poles about 3 meters long. From evening onward, this is performed by seven coastal districts in front of the romon and on the precinct approach. Other districts also hold yatai neri from daytime, with joint performances at sites including in front of JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|1=Aboshi Station}} and {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Elementary School|Q11447488|WD=Q11447488|1=Katsuhara Elementary School}}. Danjiri districts begin pulling danjiri toward the otabisho around noon. Around 22:00, three mikoshi and a gold gohei leave the main hall for the west otabisho ("Nishi no Baba") (togyo), where rites are performed (shinko-sai). ;Hirumiya (October 22) From early morning, yatai and others from each district gather at the otabisho around 8-9 a.m. for joint neri. In the afternoon, mikoshi and gold gohei return to the shrine (kango), and yatai move to the shrine in prearranged order. Highlights include yatai neri lifting floats high with the shout "Chosa," lion dance, and danjiri performances. At the 2019 hirumiya, before kango, a parade celebrating the emperor's accession was held in connection with that day's {{ill|lt=Sokuirei Seiden no Gi|Q20041008|WD=Q20041008|1=Enthronement ceremony of the Japanese Emperor}}. ;Festival areas The following 24 towns: * Himeji {{ill|lt=Otsu Ward|Q11437241|WD=Q11437241|1=Ōtsu}} - '''Yoshimi''', '''Hiramatsu''', Temma, Nagamatsu, Nishi-doi * Himeji {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Ward|Q11400189|WD=Q11400189|1=Katsuhara}} - Asahi-dani, Kumami, Miyata, Yoro * Himeji Aboshi Ward - '''Oeshima''', '''Kakichi''', '''Okinohama''', '''Shinzaike''', '''Yokohama''', Sakaide, Sakaue, Takada, Tsuichiba-kita, Tsuichiba, Fukui, Waku, Tai, Miyauchi * {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Hyogo)|Q1155186|WD=Q1155186|lt=Taishi Town|1=Taishi, Hyōgo}} - Itoi **Okinohama, Shinzaike, Yokohama, and Kakichi are danjiri districts; Asahi-dani is lion-danjiri. Miyauchi leads togyo/kango, and Waku with Ekimae conducts joint yatai procession. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:網干]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

[[File:Rōmon of Usuki Hachiman Shrine.jpg|260px]]
Romon gate |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}{{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}Miyauchi 193 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = Tsu-no-miya |札所等 = |例祭 = October 21-22 |神事 = Bushin Festival (last Saturday of March) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Usuki
Hachiman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Usuki Hachiman Shrine''' (うすきはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at Miyauchi 193, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also called "Tsu-no-miya." It is said to have been founded by divine oracle during {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. It is one of the candidate identifications for {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine|Q17226544|WD=Q17226544|1=Nakatomi Itate Shrine}}, a {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. A {{ill|lt=chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} said to have been lifted by {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Unosuke|Q11354047|WD=Q11354047|1=Sannomiya Unosuke}} (an {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} strongman/acrobat) remains, and a statue of Unosuke is also erected. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} === Festivals === * Bushin Festival - last Saturday of {{ill|lt=March|Q110|WD=Q110|1=March}} (Himeji City designated intangible folk cultural property) * Shokon Festival - April 22 * Sento Festival - {{ill|lt=July 14|Q2691|WD=Q2691|1=July 14}} * Autumn annual festival - {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}-{{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}} === Lantern Festival === The autumn annual festival is called the '''Lantern Festival'''. Eighteen yatai, four danjiri, and one lion-danjiri participate; it has the largest number of parishioners in the Banshu region{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}}, the {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Harima, has 5 yatai; {{ill|lt=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine|Q11529360|WD=Q11529360|1=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine}} known for the {{ill|lt=Nada no Kenka Matsuri|Q11566794|WD=Q11566794|1=Nada no kenka matsuri}} has 7; most other local shrines have single-digit counts, while Usuki has 23 total (yatai/danjiri/lion-danjiri).. It is one of the representative festivals of {{ill|lt=Banshu autumn festivals|Q11498705|WD=Q11498705|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri}} and is designated an intangible folk cultural property of Hyogo Prefecture (2007). The festival is said to originate from travel lanterns offered as sacred lights for three mikoshi carrying the three principal deities during their procession to the otabisho. After the war, it gradually became more intense, eventually featuring vigorous "neri" performances before shrine entry[https://www.himeji-kanko.jp/event/13 Lantern Festival ] Himeji Tourism Navi "Himenomichi". ;Yoimiya (October 21) The famous '''chochin neri''' crushes lanterns hung from tips of green bamboo poles about 3 meters long. From evening onward, this is performed by seven coastal districts in front of the romon and on the precinct approach. Other districts also hold yatai neri from daytime, with joint performances at sites including in front of JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|1=Aboshi Station}} and {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Elementary School|Q11447488|WD=Q11447488|1=Katsuhara Elementary School}}. Danjiri districts begin pulling danjiri toward the otabisho around noon. Around 22:00, three mikoshi and a gold gohei leave the main hall for the west otabisho ("Nishi no Baba") (togyo), where rites are performed (shinko-sai). ;Hirumiya (October 22) From early morning, yatai and others from each district gather at the otabisho around 8-9 a.m. for joint neri. In the afternoon, mikoshi and gold gohei return to the shrine (kango), and yatai move to the shrine in prearranged order. Highlights include yatai neri lifting floats high with the shout "Chosa," lion dance, and danjiri performances. At the 2019 hirumiya, before kango, a parade celebrating the emperor's accession was held in connection with that day's {{ill|lt=Sokuirei Seiden no Gi|Q20041008|WD=Q20041008|1=Enthronement ceremony of the Japanese Emperor}}. ;Festival areas The following 24 towns: * Himeji {{ill|lt=Otsu Ward|Q11437241|WD=Q11437241|1=Ōtsu}} - '''Yoshimi''', '''Hiramatsu''', Temma, Nagamatsu, Nishi-doi * Himeji {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Ward|Q11400189|WD=Q11400189|1=Katsuhara}} - Asahi-dani, Kumami, Miyata, Yoro * Himeji Aboshi Ward - '''Oeshima''', '''Kakichi''', '''Okinohama''', '''Shinzaike''', '''Yokohama''', Sakaide, Sakaue, Takada, Tsuichiba-kita, Tsuichiba, Fukui, Waku, Tai, Miyauchi * {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Hyogo)|Q1155186|WD=Q1155186|lt=Taishi Town|1=Taishi, Hyōgo}} - Itoi **Okinohama, Shinzaike, Yokohama, and Kakichi are danjiri districts; Asahi-dani is lion-danjiri. Miyauchi leads togyo/kango, and Waku with Ekimae conducts joint yatai procession. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:網干]]

Hachiman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Usuki Hachiman Shrine''' (うすきはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at Miyauchi 193, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also called "Tsu-no-miya." It is said to have been founded by divine oracle during {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}'s {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. It is one of the candidate identifications for {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Intatsu Shrine|Q17226544|WD=Q17226544|1=Nakatomi Itate Shrine}}, a {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. A {{ill|lt=chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} said to have been lifted by {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Unosuke|Q11354047|WD=Q11354047|1=Sannomiya Unosuke}} (an {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} strongman/acrobat) remains, and a statue of Unosuke is also erected. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} === Festivals === * Bushin Festival - last Saturday of {{ill|lt=March|Q110|WD=Q110|1=March}} (Himeji City designated intangible folk cultural property) * Shokon Festival - April 22 * Sento Festival - {{ill|lt=July 14|Q2691|WD=Q2691|1=July 14}} * Autumn annual festival - {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}-{{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|1=October 22}} === Lantern Festival === The autumn annual festival is called the '''Lantern Festival'''. Eighteen yatai, four danjiri, and one lion-danjiri participate; it has the largest number of parishioners in the Banshu region{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}}, the {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Harima, has 5 yatai; {{ill|lt=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine|Q11529360|WD=Q11529360|1=Matsubara Hachiman Shrine}} known for the {{ill|lt=Nada no Kenka Matsuri|Q11566794|WD=Q11566794|1=Nada no kenka matsuri}} has 7; most other local shrines have single-digit counts, while Usuki has 23 total (yatai/danjiri/lion-danjiri).. It is one of the representative festivals of {{ill|lt=Banshu autumn festivals|Q11498705|WD=Q11498705|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri}} and is designated an intangible folk cultural property of Hyogo Prefecture (2007). The festival is said to originate from travel lanterns offered as sacred lights for three mikoshi carrying the three principal deities during their procession to the otabisho. After the war, it gradually became more intense, eventually featuring vigorous "neri" performances before shrine entry[https://www.himeji-kanko.jp/event/13 Lantern Festival ] Himeji Tourism Navi "Himenomichi". ;Yoimiya (October 21) The famous '''chochin neri''' crushes lanterns hung from tips of green bamboo poles about 3 meters long. From evening onward, this is performed by seven coastal districts in front of the romon and on the precinct approach. Other districts also hold yatai neri from daytime, with joint performances at sites including in front of JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|1=Aboshi Station}} and {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Elementary School|Q11447488|WD=Q11447488|1=Katsuhara Elementary School}}. Danjiri districts begin pulling danjiri toward the otabisho around noon. Around 22:00, three mikoshi and a gold gohei leave the main hall for the west otabisho ("Nishi no Baba") (togyo), where rites are performed (shinko-sai). ;Hirumiya (October 22) From early morning, yatai and others from each district gather at the otabisho around 8-9 a.m. for joint neri. In the afternoon, mikoshi and gold gohei return to the shrine (kango), and yatai move to the shrine in prearranged order. Highlights include yatai neri lifting floats high with the shout "Chosa," lion dance, and danjiri performances. At the 2019 hirumiya, before kango, a parade celebrating the emperor's accession was held in connection with that day's {{ill|lt=Sokuirei Seiden no Gi|Q20041008|WD=Q20041008|1=Enthronement ceremony of the Japanese Emperor}}. ;Festival areas The following 24 towns: * Himeji {{ill|lt=Otsu Ward|Q11437241|WD=Q11437241|1=Ōtsu}} - '''Yoshimi''', '''Hiramatsu''', Temma, Nagamatsu, Nishi-doi * Himeji {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Ward|Q11400189|WD=Q11400189|1=Katsuhara}} - Asahi-dani, Kumami, Miyata, Yoro * Himeji Aboshi Ward - '''Oeshima''', '''Kakichi''', '''Okinohama''', '''Shinzaike''', '''Yokohama''', Sakaide, Sakaue, Takada, Tsuichiba-kita, Tsuichiba, Fukui, Waku, Tai, Miyauchi * {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Hyogo)|Q1155186|WD=Q1155186|lt=Taishi Town|1=Taishi, Hyōgo}} - Itoi **Okinohama, Shinzaike, Yokohama, and Kakichi are danjiri districts; Asahi-dani is lion-danjiri. Miyauchi leads togyo/kango, and Waku with Ekimae conducts joint yatai procession. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:網干]]

In the afternoon, mikoshi and gold gohei return to the shrine (kango), and yatai move to the shrine in prearranged order. Highlights include yatai neri lifting floats high with the shout "Chosa," lion dance, and danjiri performances. At the 2019 hirumiya, before kango, a parade celebrating the emperor's accession was held in connection with that day's {{ill|lt=Sokuirei Seiden no Gi|Q20041008|WD=Q20041008|1=Enthronement ceremony of the Japanese Emperor}}. ;Festival areas The following 24 towns: * Himeji {{ill|lt=Otsu Ward|Q11437241|WD=Q11437241|1=Ōtsu}} - '''Yoshimi''', '''Hiramatsu''', Temma, Nagamatsu, Nishi-doi * Himeji {{ill|lt=Katsuhara Ward|Q11400189|WD=Q11400189|1=Katsuhara}} - Asahi-dani, Kumami, Miyata, Yoro * Himeji Aboshi Ward - '''Oeshima''', '''Kakichi''', '''Okinohama''', '''Shinzaike''', '''Yokohama''', Sakaide, Sakaue, Takada, Tsuichiba-kita, Tsuichiba, Fukui, Waku, Tai, Miyauchi * {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Hyogo)|Q1155186|WD=Q1155186|lt=Taishi Town|1=Taishi, Hyōgo}} - Itoi **Okinohama, Shinzaike, Yokohama, and Kakichi are danjiri districts; Asahi-dani is lion-danjiri. Miyauchi leads togyo/kango, and Waku with Ekimae conducts joint yatai procession. Other areas use yatai. **Mikoshi and gold gohei assignments are fixed: Ojin by Okinohama, Jingu by Shinzaike, Tamayorihime by Yokohama, and gold gohei by Oeshima. **'''Bold''' are the seven chochin-neri districts. === Cultural properties[https://usukihachiman.or.jp/about_usuki/bunkazai/ Official Usuki Hachiman Shrine site (cultural properties)] === ;Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties * Romon gate * Main hall of subsidiary Shikishima Shrine ;Hyogo Prefecture designated important intangible folk cultural property * Autumn annual festival performing arts ;Himeji City designated tangible cultural property * Main hall ;Himeji City designated important intangible folk cultural property * Bushin Festival === Access === * 1 km north from {{ill|lt=Sanyo-Aboshi Station|Q28812906|WD=Q28812906|1=Sanyo-Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway|Q2245513|WD=Q2245513|lt=Sanyo Dentetsu|1=Sanyo Electric Railway}}. * 2.5 km south from JR {{ill|lt=Aboshi Station|Q3611088|WD=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on {{ill|lt=Sanyo Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}; 5 minutes by taxi. === Footnotes === === External links === {{Commonscat|Usukihachiman-jinja(Himeji City)}} * [http://www.usukihachiman.or.jp/ Usuki Hachiman Shrine Official Picture Scroll] * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/shisei/0000007131.html Himeji Festivals] Himeji City === See also === * {{ill|lt=Banshu Autumn Festival Series|Q11498704|WD=Q11498704|1=Banshu Autumn Festival Series}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Usuki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:姫路市の神社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:網干]]
